content.text: Chat UI Components for Claude in React Native

A chat interface is the most natural UI for a Claude-powered mobile app. React Native offers two viable paths: the GiftedChat library, which provides pre-built bubbles, timestamps, avatars, and an input bar in minutes; or a fully custom FlatList-based component that gives you precise control over every visual and behavioral detail. Both paths integrate with the Anthropic SDK's streaming API, but they require different integration patterns and trade off setup speed against flexibility.

attrs.alt: GiftedChat vs custom FlatList: two mobile chat UI approaches for Claude

The Anthropic API requires the full conversation history on every request — it is stateless. Build the history from your message array, excluding the placeholder streaming message (which has empty

on every chunk can cause janky scroll behavior.
GiftedChat provides avatars, timestamps, keyboard handling, and bubble layout in minutes — best for prototypes. Custom FlatList gives complete visual and behavioral control at the cost of more code.
Both approaches use the same streaming pattern: create a placeholder message, update it with a functional
The API is stateless — always re-send the full conversation history, filtering out in-progress placeholders.
Cancel with AbortController and clean up on component unmount to prevent setState-after-unmount errors.
Long conversations grow token usage linearly per turn — implement a sliding window or server-side summarization early.
